question.label: Growth blockers
question.name: What's in your way of getting to the next level — not what's missing, what's actively blocking?
question.description: Forces specificity about obstacles rather than vague 'more opportunities please'.

answer: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.

assessment.note: He names a specific blocker to next-level scope — staying in execution details — which is a strong growth constraint with some self-awareness about the gap.

new_queue.label: Execution details
new_queue.name: Execution details — what keeps pulling you back into them?

new_queue.label: Stretch now
new_queue.name: Which part of your current work is actually stretching you right now?

new_queue.label: Senior scope now
new_queue.name: Where does your work feel most like Head of Product work right now?

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Role definition
new_queue.name: What does the next-level Head of Product role mean to you?

new_queue.label: Trust gaps
new_queue.name: Where does stakeholder trust feel thinnest for you today?

new_queue.label: Delegate more
new_queue.name: What are you still holding that someone else could own?

new_queue.label: Manager support
new_queue.name: What would help you spend more time on strategic decisions than delivery details?

new_queue.label: Next experiment
new_queue.name: What's one concrete shift you want to try before our next conversation?

history.answer_excerpt: I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
